channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools like GitHub and Notion, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas promises valuable in-person experiences for developers, focusing on agentic AI and various technical tracks.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that enhances TensorFlow with faster processing and better support for multiple programming languages.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on practice.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: Gemini Code Assist has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows, making the AI a more effective collaborator for developers.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: New features in Gemini Code Assist for IntelliJ and VS Code aim to reduce developer friction by automating code completion and providing interactive summaries.

item.title: Forget Shokz: I tried the Suunto Spark earbuds for a month, and they've sold me on air conduction
item.description: A review of Suunto's air conduction earbuds reveals they outperform bone conduction headphones, especially during workouts.

item.title: iOS 26.4 brings essential upgrades to your iPhone - including a vital security fix
item.description: iOS 26.4 introduces crucial upgrades for iPhones, including a significant security fix, though an improved Siri is still forthcoming.

item.title: YouTube Premium's price goes up to $15.99 in June - but you can save $32 with one change
item.description: YouTube Premium will increase its price to $15.99 in June, but users can save money by adjusting their plans.

item.title: Microsoft's Copilot strategy is just more user abuse from Redmond, says Mozilla
item.description: Mozilla criticizes Microsoft's Copilot strategy, claiming it prioritizes AI features over user choice. The company argues this reflects a troubling trend in tech practices.

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: A stalking victim is suing OpenAI, alleging that ChatGPT exacerbated her abuser's delusions and ignored multiple warnings about his dangerous behavior.

item.title: Your router may be vulnerable to Russian hackers, FBI warns: 5 steps to take now
item.description: The FBI and NSA warn that Russian hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in routers, advising users to take specific protective measures to secure their devices.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcases advancements in physical AI, highlighting how robots are transforming various industries through improved learning and simulation.

item.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
item.description: TechCrunch is set to host the Startup Battlefield in Tokyo, focusing on AI, robotics, and other transformative technologies with live demonstrations and discussions.

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
item.description: A suspect was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, highlighting security concerns for tech leaders.

item.title: I walked 3,000 steps with my Apple Watch, Google Pixel, and Oura Ring - this tracker was most accurate
item.description: A comparison of three health trackers revealed varying accuracy in step counting, providing insights for consumers on wearable technology.

item.title: This Startup Wants You to Pay Up to Talk With AI Versions of Human Experts
item.description: Onix is launching a platform that allows users to interact with AI versions of human experts, blending advice with product promotion.

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: A tutorial for beginners on using GitHub Copilot CLI is provided, guiding users through the initial steps of this coding assistant tool.

item.title: Electronics industry says FCC's foreign-made router policy is a bit of a mesh
item.description: The Global Electronics Association criticizes the FCC's policy on foreign-made routers, arguing it limits consumer choices and delays access to new technology.

item.title: How I check, test, and charge my AA and AAA batteries - and sift out defective ones
item.description: The article discusses the author's experience with Olight's new battery charger, highlighting its effectiveness in testing and charging AA and AAA batteries. This pro-grade charger is praised for its reliability and performance.

item.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
item.description: Moderna faces a dilemma over whether to label its new mRNA treatments as vaccines or therapies, impacting public perception and acceptance. This confusion arises amid ongoing vaccine skepticism.

item.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
item.description: TechCrunch is offering a limited-time discount of up to $500 on passes for its Disrupt 2026 event, with the offer ending tonight. This is a final call for potential attendees to save money.

item.title: My favorite Android Auto find is these hidden shortcuts that are highly customizable
item.description: The article reveals hidden customizable shortcuts in Android Auto that enhance user experience. These shortcuts can be set up quickly, making navigation more efficient.

item.title: The Iranian Lego AI video creators credit their virality to ‘heart’
item.description: Iranian content creators have gained attention for their viral AI-generated Lego videos, which satirically depict political events, contrasting with mainstream narratives. This reflects a unique use of AI in storytelling.

item.title: CPUID site hijacked to serve malware instead of HWMonitor downloads
item.description: The CPUID website was briefly compromised, redirecting users to malware instead of legitimate downloads. This incident raises concerns about cybersecurity and trust in online resources.

item.title: Amazon would rather shareholders did not look too closely at carbon footprint
item.description: Amazon's board is advising shareholders to reject a proposal for greater transparency regarding the environmental impact of its AWS expansion. This decision raises questions about corporate accountability in climate issues.

item.title: Suits won't quit AI spending, even if they can't prove it's working
item.description: Despite uncertainty in measuring AI's effectiveness, 65% of UK business leaders plan to continue investing in AI as a strategic tool for transformation. This reflects a long-term commitment to AI technologies.

item.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
item.description: The Download newsletter features an exclusive short story by Jeff VanderMeer and discusses AI models that are considered too dangerous to release. This highlights ongoing concerns about AI safety.

item.title: Project Glasswing and open source software: The good, the bad, and the ugly
item.description: Project Glasswing, backed by tech giants, aims to use AI to identify and fix vulnerabilities in open source software. This initiative addresses critical security concerns in the software community.

item.title: Britain seeks views before it drops the hammer on signal jammers
item.description: The UK government is seeking public input on proposed legislation to ban radiofrequency jammers, devices often linked to criminal activities. This four-week call for evidence aims to shape effective laws around these controversial devices.

item.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
item.description: A New Yorker article explores the tumultuous leadership of Sam Altman at OpenAI, detailing his brief firing and subsequent reinstatement as CEO. The piece raises questions about the future direction of the organization under his leadership.

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: OpenAI has supported an Illinois bill that would limit the liability of AI labs in cases of mass harm or financial disasters caused by their products. This move could significantly impact accountability in the AI industry.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new $100/month subscription plan for ChatGPT, catering to power users who require more extensive access. This plan offers a significant increase in usage compared to previous tiers.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: The Florida Attorney General is investigating OpenAI following a shooting incident where ChatGPT was allegedly used to plan the attack. The victim's family intends to sue OpenAI, raising concerns about AI's role in violent acts.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new feature allowing admins and security managers to use Copilot directly from security assessment results, providing contextual guidance for risk management. This aims to enhance security workflows.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to help businesses automate ongoing tasks using AI agents. This development aims to simplify operations for organizations looking to enhance efficiency.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta's AI app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store following the launch of its Muse Spark model, up from No. 57. This rapid rise indicates growing interest in Meta's AI offerings.

item.title: Microsoft named a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Sovereign Cloud Platforms
item.description: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Sovereign Cloud Platforms, highlighting its leadership in addressing evolving sovereignty needs in cloud services.

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a new $100 per month Pro subscription for ChatGPT, offering five times more usage of its Codex coding tool compared to the $20 plan. This tier is aimed at users requiring extensive coding support.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has restricted the release of its AI model, Mythos, citing concerns over its potential to expose security vulnerabilities. This raises questions about whether the decision is genuinely for cybersecurity or to protect the company’s interests.

item.title: promptscan added to PyPI
item.description: Promptscan, a new tool for detecting and analyzing suspicious prompts, has been added to the Python Package Index (PyPI). This tool aims to enhance security in AI interactions.

item.title: mcparmory-netlicensing added to PyPI
item.description: The mcparmory-netlicensing package has been introduced to PyPI, providing a server for the Labs64 NetLicensing RESTful API Test Center. This tool aims to streamline licensing processes.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub has introduced a 'Low Quality' option for moderators to classify hidden comments, enhancing moderation tools across various features. This aims to improve the quality of discussions on the platform.

item.title: Claude Mythos Will Uplevel AI Again and Exposes and Will Try to Fix Cybersecurity Issues
item.description: Anthropic's new AI model, Mythos, is set to significantly enhance capabilities compared to its predecessor, Claude Opus 4.6. The model aims to address cybersecurity issues while pushing AI boundaries.

item.title: bernstein 1.5.16
item.description: The bernstein 1.5.16 update introduces declarative agent orchestration aimed at improving workflows for engineering teams. This tool is designed to streamline project management.

item.title: photoscaler added to PyPI
item.description: Photoscaler, a new library for professional image and video scaling with audio support, has been added to PyPI. This tool aims to improve media processing capabilities.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: A guide on managing the lifecycle of models in Amazon Bedrock has been published, detailing strategies for smooth transitions as models evolve. This is crucial for maintaining operational AI applications.

item.title: AWS previews a cloud-agnostic registry for managing agentic fleets at scale
item.description: AWS has previewed a cloud-agnostic registry aimed at managing AI agents at scale, addressing the challenges of 'agentic sprawl' in enterprise environments. This initiative seeks to streamline AI deployment.

item.title: flyteplugins-pytorch 2.1.5
item.description: The flyteplugins-pytorch 2.1.5 update introduces a new plugin for integrating PyTorch with Flyte, enhancing the capabilities for machine learning workflows.
